purpose: Defines every accepted structured spell markdown field before it is translated into runtime JSON.

notes: Structured markdown is the first machine-actionable layer. If a spell needs a fact that this template cannot express, the template must grow before the spell is flattened into JSON.
Every field has a jsonPath or intentionally empty jsonPath so drift between structured markdown and runtime JSON is visible.

log.why: Spell level is a universal spell identity field.

fields.comment: Spell level is a universal spell identity field. It maps to `level`. fields.label: Targeting Max

log.why: Many spells have explicit target counts, and some scale those counts.

fields.comment: Many spells have explicit target counts, some scale those counts, and a small number say any number of targets. It maps to `targeting.maxTargets`. Purpose: Targeting Max exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the canonical markdown prose, including mechanics hidden in descriptive sentences. Use a finite number for fixed caps, a scalable object when slot or character level changes the cap, and `unlimited` only when the canonical prose truly has no numeric cap. It maps to runtime JSON path `targeting.maxTargets`. The value type is `number-scalable-or-unlimited`. This is optional or conditional in the runtime shape. Omit or leave empty only when this runtime/template layer intentionally treats the field as optional.

acceptedValueNotes.number: Use a finite number when the spell gives an exact target cap.
acceptedValueNotes.scalable: Use a scalable object when character level or slot level changes the target cap.
acceptedValueNotes.unlimited: Use `unlimited` only when the canonical spell prose allows any number of valid targets; do not use a fake high number such as 999.

fields.label: Target Participation Requires Full Casting Range

log.why: Prayer of Healing chooses targets for a long casting, but those targets only qualify if they stay within range until the casting finishes.

fields.comment: Target Participation Requires Full Casting Range records whether selected targets must remain inside the spell's range for the whole casting time. Use `true` for Prayer of Healing-style wording, where the chosen creatures must stay within range for the entire ten-minute casting before they receive the effect. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no long-casting target-participation rule.

fields.label: Target Participation Notes

log.why: The typed participation flag sometimes needs a short reminder of the exact casting-time wording.

fields.comment: Target Participation Notes preserves a short canonical reminder for long-casting target participation. It should clarify the typed field rather than replace it; for example, that selected targets must remain within 30 feet for the entire casting.

fields.label: Target Cluster Requirement

log.why: Some higher-slot multi-target spells require all chosen targets to be within a shared distance of each other.

fields.comment: Target Cluster Requirement records whether selected targets must satisfy a mutual-distance rule. Use `required` when canonical prose says the chosen targets must be within a distance of each other; use `not_applicable` only when this field is carried as an explicit sentinel. It maps to runtime JSON path `targeting.targetCluster.requirement`.

acceptedValueNotes.required: Use when all chosen targets must be close enough to each other for the cast to be legal.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use only when the spell has no mutual-distance target cluster rule.

fields.label: Target Cluster Max Distance

log.why: Mutual-distance target cluster rules need a numeric distance instead of prose-only scaling notes.

fields.comment: Target Cluster Max Distance stores the maximum allowed separation between selected targets for spells with a target-cluster rule. It maps to runtime JSON path `targeting.targetCluster.maxDistance`. Use the numeric canonical distance, such as 30, or `not_applicable` when there is no target-cluster mechanic.

fields.label: Target Cluster Distance Unit

log.why: Target-cluster distance needs its unit recorded alongside the number.

fields.comment: Target Cluster Distance Unit stores the unit for Target Cluster Max Distance. It maps to runtime JSON path `targeting.targetCluster.distanceUnit`. Use `feet` for rules such as within 30 feet of each other, and `not_applicable` when there is no target-cluster mechanic.

fields.label: Target Cluster Scope

log.why: The template needs to say whether the cluster rule applies to all chosen targets or another future subset.

fields.comment: Target Cluster Scope records which selected targets must satisfy the cluster rule. The current supported value `all_targets` means every chosen target must be within the recorded distance relationship. It maps to runtime JSON path `targeting.targetCluster.scope`.

acceptedValueNotes.all_targets: Use when every selected target must satisfy the mutual-distance rule.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use only when the spell has no target-cluster mechanic.

fields.label: Target Cluster Notes

log.why: Short prose notes preserve exact canonical wording while the typed target-cluster fields carry runtime behavior.

fields.comment: Target Cluster Notes stores a short clarification for the target-cluster rule. It should quote or paraphrase only the relevant mechanic, not replace the typed requirement, distance, unit, and scope fields.

fields.label: Area Target Selection Mode

log.why: Some area spells affect only creatures the caster chooses inside the footprint rather than every valid creature in the area.

fields.comment: Area Target Selection Mode records whether an area affects every valid target, caster-chosen targets, random targets, or no area-selection mechanic. It maps to `targeting.areaTargetSelection.mode`. Use `caster_choice` for phrases such as 'each creature of your choice in the area'. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no area-target-selection mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.all_valid_targets: Use when every valid target in the area is affected.
acceptedValueNotes.caster_choice: Use when the caster selects which valid creatures inside the area are affected.
acceptedValueNotes.random: Use only when canonical prose randomizes affected targets inside an area.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell does not have an area-target-selection mechanic.

fields.label: Area Target Selection Scope

log.why: Selected-area targeting needs to say what population is being selected from.

fields.comment: Area Target Selection Scope names the population the selection is drawn from. This first pass supports `creatures_in_area`; use `not_applicable` for spells without area target selection.

acceptedValueNotes.creatures_in_area: Use when the caster chooses affected creatures that are already inside the spell area.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell does not have an area-target-selection mechanic.

fields.label: Area Target Selection Count

log.why: Phrases like 'each creature of your choice' need a schema-safe value that does not invent a numeric cap.

fields.comment: Area Target Selection Count records whether the chosen area targets are uncapped (`all_chosen`), numerically capped, or not applicable. Use `all_chosen` when the prose says each chosen creature and gives no maximum count.

acceptedValueNotes.all_chosen: Use when the caster may choose any number of valid creatures in the area.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell does not have an area-target-selection mechanic.

fields.label: Area Target Selection Excludes Unchosen

log.why: Caster-selected area spells need to distinguish unchosen valid creatures from affected targets.

fields.comment: Area Target Selection Excludes Unchosen is true when valid creatures inside the area are unaffected unless the caster selected them. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no area-target-selection mechanic.

fields.label: Area Target Selection Requires Line Of Sight

log.why: Some selected-area spells require the caster to see the chosen creatures inside the area.

fields.comment: Area Target Selection Requires Line Of Sight is true when the caster can only select creatures they can see inside the area. Use `not_applicable` when the selected-area mechanic does not add a sight gate or when the spell has no area-target-selection mechanic.

fields.label: Area Target Selection Notes

log.why: Selected-area targeting sometimes needs a short prose note while the runtime fields remain narrow.

fields.comment: Area Target Selection Notes preserves short clarifying text for selected-area targeting without replacing the typed mode, scope, count, and exclusion fields.

fields.label: Target Instance Type

log.why: Some spells create multiple assignable units, such as Magic Missile darts, that are not the same as max target count.

fields.comment: Target Instance Type names the repeated unit the caster assigns among targets or positions. Use `dart` for Magic Missile, `beam` for Eldritch Blast, `ray` for Scorching Ray, `projectile` for planted ammunition, `light` for Dancing Lights-style light instances, `animated_tree` for Druid Grove-style awakened-tree guardians, and `controlled_undead` for Animate Dead-style reasserted undead targets. Use `not_applicable` when the spell does not create assignable target instances.

fields.label: Target Instance Base Count

fields.label: Secondary Target Trigger

fields.comment: Secondary Target Trigger records what event allows a secondary or chained target to be chosen. fields.label: Secondary Target Origin

log.why: Secondary target range is usually measured from another target rather than from the caster.

fields.comment: Secondary Target Origin records the point the secondary range is measured from. Use `primary_target` for Green-Flame Blade-style one-step leaps, `previous_target` for chains that keep jumping from the latest target, and `not_applicable` when there is no secondary target mechanic.

fields.label: Secondary Target Range

log.why: Secondary target relationships need their own range separate from the initial spell range.

fields.comment: Secondary Target Range stores the maximum distance from the secondary target origin to the follow-up target. Use the numeric canonical distance, or `not_applicable` when there is no secondary target mechanic.

fields.label: Secondary Target Range Unit

log.why: Secondary target range needs an explicit unit.

fields.comment: Secondary Target Range Unit stores the unit for Secondary Target Range. Use `feet` for current leap and chain mechanics, and `not_applicable` when there is no secondary target mechanic.

fields.label: Secondary Target Valid Targets

log.why: Follow-up target eligibility can differ from the spell's initial target eligibility.

fields.comment: Secondary Target Valid Targets records what the follow-up target may be. Use `creature` when the prose says creature, `creature_or_object` only when the prose permits either, and `not_applicable` when there is no secondary target mechanic.

fields.label: Secondary Target Selection

log.why: The runtime needs to know who chooses the secondary target.

fields.comment: Secondary Target Selection records who chooses the follow-up target. Use `caster_choice` for current leap/chain spells and `not_applicable` when there is no secondary target mechanic.

fields.label: Secondary Target Must Be Different

fields.comment: Secondary Target Must Be Different is true when canonical prose requires a different target or creature from the origin. Use `not_applicable` when there is no secondary target mechanic.

fields.label: Secondary Target Requires Line Of Sight

fields.comment: Secondary Target Requires Line Of Sight is true when the secondary target selection itself has a sight requirement. Use false when the prose has no sight gate, and `not_applicable` when there is no secondary target mechanic.

fields.label: Secondary Target Requires Attack Roll

fields.comment: Secondary Target Requires Attack Roll is true when the follow-up target needs a fresh attack roll. Use false for automatic rider damage and `not_applicable` when there is no secondary target mechanic.

fields.label: Secondary Target Requires Damage Roll

fields.comment: Secondary Target Requires Damage Roll is true when canonical prose says to make a new damage roll. Use false for fixed rider damage and `not_applicable` when there is no secondary target mechanic.

fields.label: Secondary Target Repeat Rule

fields.comment: Secondary Target Repeat Rule records whether the follow-up can chain again. Use `none` for one-time secondary targets, `slot_level_max_leaps` when the slot level sets the maximum number of leaps, and `not_applicable` when there is no secondary target mechanic.

fields.comment: Secondary Target Max Leaps records the maximum number of follow-up leaps. Use `slot_level` when the maximum equals the spell slot level expended, or `not_applicable` when the secondary target cannot repeat.

fields.label: Secondary Target Unique Per Casting

fields.comment: Secondary Target Unique Per Casting is true when each creature can be targeted only once by the casting. Use `not_applicable` when there is no secondary target mechanic.

fields.label: Defense Value

log.why: AC, temporary hit point, and similar defensive numbers must be explicit.

fields.comment: AC, temporary hit point, and similar defensive numbers must be explicit. It maps to `effects[].value`. Purpose: Defense Value exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the canonical markdown prose, including mechanics hidden in descriptive sentences. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].value`. The value type is `number-or-formula`. fields.label: Condition Immunities

log.why: Heroism showed that condition immunity is a defensive mechanic distinct from damage immunity and should not remain utility prose.

fields.comment: Lists named status conditions the spell prevents or makes the target immune to while the defensive effect is active. Use this for condition immunity such as Frightened; use damageType only for resistance or immunity to damage types. It maps to `effects[].conditionImmunity[]`. Purpose: Condition Immunities exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the canonical markdown prose, including mechanics hidden in descriptive sentences. The value type is `csv-string`. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no condition-immunity mechanic.

fields.label: Prevention Immunities

log.why: Aura of Life prevents hit point maximum reduction, which is neither a condition immunity nor damage resistance.

fields.comment: Lists non-condition mechanics the spell prevents while a defensive effect is active. Use this for prevention wording such as `hit point maximum can't be reduced`; use Condition Immunities for named conditions and Damage Type for damage resistance or immunity. It maps to `effects[].preventionImmunity[]`. Purpose: Prevention Immunities keeps non-condition prevention mechanics from being hidden in utility prose. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no such prevention mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.hit_point_maximum_reduction: The protected target's hit point maximum cannot be reduced.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: The spell has no non-condition prevention-immunity mechanic.

fields.label: Damage Type Exclusions

log.why: Feign Death grants resistance to all damage except Psychic, which needs a typed exception list instead of prose-only handling.

fields.comment: Lists damage types excluded from a broad defensive damage entry. Use this when canonical prose says a target resists or is immune to a large damage set with named exceptions, such as `all damage except Psychic`. It maps to `effects[].excludedDamageType[]`. Purpose: Damage Type Exclusions exists so exception-based resistance/immunity is not flattened into description prose or misrepresented as unconditional all-damage protection. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no damage-type exception mechanic.

fields.label: Defense Damage Type Source

log.why: Absorb Elements lists eligible damage types, but the actual resistance is copied from the triggering damage event.

fields.comment: Explains how to interpret defensive `Damage Type` entries. Use `listed` when every listed type applies directly, `triggering_damage_type` when the active type is copied from the damage event that triggered the spell, and `chosen_damage_type` when a caster or player chooses one type from the listed options. It maps to `effects[].damageTypeSource`. Purpose: Defense Damage Type Source prevents dynamic resistance from being mistaken for simultaneous resistance to every eligible listed type. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no defensive damage-type mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.listed: Every listed Damage Type applies directly.
acceptedValueNotes.triggering_damage_type: The active type is the damage type from the event that triggered the spell.
acceptedValueNotes.chosen_damage_type: The active type is selected by the caster/player from the eligible list.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: The spell has no defensive damage-type source mechanic.

fields.label: Damage Reduction Dice

log.why: Resistance reduces qualifying damage by 1d4, which is not the same mechanic as resistance or immunity.

fields.comment: Stores the rolled amount subtracted from qualifying damage. Use this for damage-reduction mechanics such as `reduce the total damage taken by 1d4`; do not use it for normal resistance or immunity. It maps to `effects[].damageReduction.dice`. Purpose: Damage Reduction Dice exists so damage subtraction is not flattened into utility prose or mis-modeled as resistance. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no rolled damage-reduction mechanic.

fields.label: Damage Reduction Applies To

log.why: Damage reduction needs to say whether it subtracts from incoming damage, outgoing damage, or another future damage total.

fields.comment: Names which damage total the reduction modifies. Current closed corpus usage is `damage_taken`, meaning the target subtracts the amount from incoming damage it takes. It maps to `effects[].damageReduction.appliesTo`. Purpose: Damage Reduction Applies To prevents reduction rules from being confused with attack damage bonuses or healing. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no damage-reduction mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.damage_taken: Subtract from damage the protected target takes.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: The spell has no damage-reduction mechanic.

fields.label: Damage Reduction Frequency

fields.why: The runtime needs to know whether sensory variants are driven by a mode menu or by a single effect.
fields.comment: Records where the sensory variant list comes from. fields.why: Mode-based sensory spells need an explicit count so missing variants do not hide inside prose.

It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sensoryManifestation.variants.length`. The value type is `integer-or-not-applicable`. Use `not_applicable` when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

fields.pattern: ^Sensory Manifestation Variant \d+ (Label|Allowed Senses|Excluded Senses|Volume Range|Timing|Max Shape|Max Size|Max Size Unit)$
fields.why: Each sensory variant needs the same repeated field set so option-specific sound, sight, light, smell, timing, and size rules can be audited.
fields.comment: Numbered Sensory Manifestation Variant fields record one spell-created sensory branch at a time. Use Label for the canonical option name; Allowed Senses for what it can create; Excluded Senses for explicit negative limits; Volume Range and Timing for sound-specific rules; and Max Shape/Max Size/Max Size Unit for image or area limits. Purpose: this numbered family keeps mode-specific sensory mechanics machine-readable without creating a new top-level field for every possible option count. Runtime mapping: `effects[].sensoryManifestation.variants[]`.

fields.why: Discerning an illusion may affect only the observer rather than ending the spell globally.

fields.label: Illusion Reveal Methods

fields.why: Different reveal methods have different runtime triggers and costs.
fields.comment: Records the methods that can reveal or discern an illusion. fields.label: Illusion Reveal Action Cost

fields.comment: Records the action cost for intentional illusion examination. Purpose: Illusion Reveal Action Cost exists so Study action reveal stays tied to turn economy. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.revealRules[].actionCost`. The value type is `enum`. Use `not_applicable` when this does not apply.

fields.label: Illusion Reveal DC

acceptedValueNotes.spell_save_dc: Use when the reveal check is made against the caster's spell save DC.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no DC applies.

fields.why: Reveal checks need a DC source, not just prose saying the creature can identify the illusion.
fields.comment: Records the DC source for a reveal check. Purpose: Illusion Reveal DC exists so Study-based reveal checks can use the same spell save DC as canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.revealRules[].dc`. The value type is `enum`. Use `not_applicable` when this does not apply.

fields.label: Illusion Reveal Applies To

fields.comment: Records which illusion or sensory variants a reveal method applies to. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.revealRules[].appliesTo`. The value type is `csv-string`. Use `not_applicable` only when there is no reveal method.

fields.label: Illusion Discerned State

acceptedValueNotes.faint_to_discerning_creature: Use when a discerned illusion becomes faint only to the creature that discerned it.

fields.comment: Records what the illusion becomes to a creature that discerns it. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.discernedState`. The value type is `enum`. Use `not_applicable` when this does not apply.
